$comment: Style-gate routes manifest (Phase 1 of the CI style-gating plan).
One entry per route the headless capture renders. The capture spec
(style-capture.spec.ts) produces, per route, a UI-Bridge snapshot JSON
(.artifacts/snapshots/<id>.json) and a deterministic PNG screenshot
(.artifacts/frames/<id>.png); a later phase feeds both to the
vision-audit analyzer bin.
ALL seed routes are AUTHENTICATED. The capture's snapshot path
(/control/snapshot via the relay) requires the in-page CommandRelayListener,
and that listener returns null without a resolved {userId, sessionId}
(provider.tsx commandRelayRegistrationMetadata) -- so a public/unauthenticated
route (e.g. /login) can NEVER serve /control/snapshot and would always 503.
Public/unauthenticated routes therefore need a relay-INDEPENDENT snapshot
path (a Playwright in-page UI-Bridge SDK eval, not the relay proxy) -- that's
deferred to a later phase. The `public` field is RETAINED in the schema for
that future path; today every route is `public: false`.
Fields:
id          filesystem-safe slug, also the artifact basename.
path        route path passed to page.goto().
public      true  -> unauthenticated route. RESERVED for a future
relay-independent capture path; NOT supported today (the relay
snapshot route requires an authenticated user+session). Keep
false for all current routes.
false -> rendered WITH the setup-minted storageState (style-gate project).
settleMs    extra wait after networkidle before capture; the top flake
source is late-mounting content, so bump this per route as
burn-in reveals slow surfaces.
description human note on what the route is.
Deliberately SMALL high-value seed set (3 authed routes). The set EXPANDS
after burn-in once the capture+baseline loop is proven stable in CI
(Phases 2-4). Add routes by appending objects here -- no code change required.

routes.description: Authenticated Home landing (src/app/(app)/co-pilot/page.tsx). An authed visit to / forwards here (use-landing-page.ts) -- this IS the post-login Home surface. RESOLVED FLAKE (was CAPTURE-UNAVAILABLE): the 503 NO_BROWSER_CONNECTED was NOT co-pilot-page-specific -- it was a COLD-START race on whichever route renders FIRST (co-pilot is first here; workers=1, serial). A fresh `npm run dev` server compiles the (app) layout/providers + this route + the /api/ui-bridge/control/snapshot relay-proxy route ON FIRST HIT, the client-side auth context keeps the (app) layout's SHARED AuthLoadingShell ('Loading...') up -- WITHHOLDING the UI-Bridge provider subtree incl. CommandRelayListener -- until the cold auth round-trip resolves, and only THEN can the relay attach. That cold compile+auth+attach sum exceeds the 60s per-test budget, so co-pilot's failure-time snapshot showed the shared AuthLoadingShell still up at 60s while the warm routes after it captured in ~20s. Proven via run 27000271391 artifacts: co-pilot diag usersMe=200 / looksLikeLogin=false (auth fine), snapshot = 'img + Loading...' (the shared shell), 1.0m timeout in captureSnapshot; build-workflows 20.8s + library 15.9s green. The EARLIER fix's per-test waitForAuthedShell (45s, silently swallowed) made it worse by eating the budget waiting for a shell that never cleared, and its beforeAll warmup only waited for the shell (also swallowed) so it never drove the relay to attach. The CURRENT fix makes beforeAll run the FULL readiness dance (navigate -> shell clears -> co-pilot ready badge -> RELAY ATTACHES) and BLOCKS on the relay actually attaching, in the no-per-test-cap hook (180s) -- so the cold compile+auth+attach happen ONCE before any per-test budget, leaving the first real test as warm as the others. Per-test waitForAuthedShell is now a short 20s (the warm path clears fast) so the bulk of the 60s budget is left for the relay poll, and co-pilot additionally waits for its own CoPilotReadyStatus badge (co-pilot-specific readiness). settleMs stays the standard 3000 -- readiness is signal-driven, not timer-driven.
Authenticated workflow builder (src/app/(app)/build/workflows/page.tsx). /dashboard redirect-shim resolves here in ai product-mode (dashboard/page.tsx).
Authenticated Library landing (src/app/(app)/library/page.tsx). Self-contained category-card grid -- no RequireProject gate, so it renders a stable content surface regardless of selected project.
